source: DT-skill-loop-restructure accepted by user in chat

loop_start.reason: Compiler loops start from source refs and build handoffs, not prior loop chat memory.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

validation.reason: Fresh validation is preferred; policy may require it for high-risk semantic gates.

next_loop.reason: The next compiler loop should start from the build handoff, not the producing compiler context.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

requirements.text: Main CodeWiki skill must stay lean and own bootstrap/status/router behavior, while compiler/gateway loops become focused agent skills with precise descriptions.
Agent-facing CodeWiki tool behavior must be source-owned under `src/application/tools/**`, with adapters acting as thin transport layers.
Each compiler/gateway loop must be improved through an atomic roadmap task defining agent actions, available tools, build compilation point, validation point, stop rules, and output contract.

evidence_mapping.criterion: User approved all semantic diff rows.
evidence_mapping.evidence: Chat approval plus `codewiki_diff_table` accepted DTR-001 through DTR-004.

evidence_mapping.criterion: External skill best-practice research supports focused workflow skills and progressive disclosure.
evidence_mapping.evidence: Research sources: Agent Skills specification, Agent Skills integration guide, Claude skill authoring best practices, Claude Code skills docs.

summary: Accepted skill restructuring direction: keep main CodeWiki skill as bootstrap/status/router, split compiler/gateway loops into dedicated focused skills, move agent-facing tool contracts under application tools, and track loop improvements as atomic tasks.

diff_table.current_state: One public `codewiki` skill routes to internal loop/playbook markdown assets under `skills/codewiki/**`.
diff_table.desired_state: Keep `codewiki` as lean bootstrap/status/router skill; expose each compiler/gateway loop as its own dedicated skill with specific trigger description and loop-local tool guidance.
diff_table.rationale: Skill best practices favor focused repeatable workflows, specific descriptions, and progressive disclosure. Separate loop skills reduce wrong-mode behavior and make each development stage stronger.
diff_table.risk: medium: more public skills add catalog tokens and require precise descriptions/tests to avoid activation collisions.

diff_table.current_state: Bootstrap guidance lives as `skills/codewiki/bootstrap/**` assets consumed by source orchestration, while main skill only points at them.
diff_table.desired_state: Consolidate bootstrap/status/onboarding guidance into the main `codewiki` skill and references while keeping detailed assets package-local and source-owned orchestration intact.
diff_table.rationale: Bootstrap is the CodeWiki entry point, not a compiler loop; it belongs in the main skill as the first user-facing mode.
diff_table.risk: low: mostly content/structure, but package asset paths and prompt tests must stay green.

diff_table.current_state: Agent-facing tool behavior is scattered across Pi adapter files and general application modules; `src/application/tools/**` contains only audit.
diff_table.desired_state: Expose source-owned application tool contracts for all `codewiki_*` operations under `src/application/tools/**`; Pi adapter stays thin transport/schema/project-resolution layer.
diff_table.rationale: Skills should call stable application tools, not adapter-owned behavior. This strengthens cross-adapter consistency and makes loop guidance testable.
diff_table.risk: medium: refactor can affect registered tools and smoke tests.

diff_table.current_state: Loop assets do not uniformly define agent actions, exact tools, build compilation point, validation point, stop rules, or output contract.
diff_table.desired_state: Create one roadmap task per loop (`feedback`, `documentation`, `planning`, `implementation`, `validation`) to perfect loop-specific mode guidance and production-grade build/gateway evidence.
diff_table.rationale: Each loop has different context, tools, build, and gateway rules; separate tasks keep work atomic and prevent broad skill churn.
diff_table.risk: medium: loop instructions must remain aligned with compiler/gateway specs and avoid duplication/drift.

accepted_decisions.summary: Keep `codewiki` as lean bootstrap/status/router skill; expose each compiler/gateway loop as its own dedicated skill with specific trigger description and loop-local tool guidance.
Consolidate bootstrap/status/onboarding guidance into the main `codewiki` skill and references while keeping detailed assets package-local and source-owned orchestration intact.
Expose source-owned application tool contracts for all `codewiki_*` operations under `src/application/tools/**`; Pi adapter stays thin transport/schema/project-resolution layer.
Create one roadmap task per loop (`feedback`, `documentation`, `planning`, `implementation`, `validation`) to perfect loop-specific mode guidance and production-grade build/gateway evidence.

open_questions: Exact public skill names and descriptions should be finalized in the skill topology task.
non_goals: Do not implement skill restructure in this feedback cycle.
Do not create umbrella/container tasks that only close other tasks.
